metadata.description: Clinical knowledge base for rare/underdiagnosed disease detection

cushings_syndrome.description: Excess cortisol production causing a constellation of metabolic symptoms often mistaken for common conditions.
red_flags: Hypertension resistant to 3+ medications
Diabetes with unexplained weight gain
Central obesity with muscle weakness
Purple striae wider than 1cm
Easy bruising without trauma

addisons_disease.description: Primary adrenal insufficiency causing chronic fatigue, hypotension, and electrolyte disturbances, frequently misdiagnosed as depression or chronic fatigue syndrome.
red_flags: Unexplained hypotension especially postural
Hyperpigmentation of skin creases and mucosa
Salt craving with fatigue
Hyponatremia + hyperkalemia combination
Fatigue unresponsive to treatment

primary_hyperaldosteronism.description: Excess aldosterone production causing secondary hypertension. Present in up to 10% of hypertensive patients but frequently dismissed as essential hypertension.
red_flags: Hypertension requiring 3+ medications
Hypertension with spontaneous hypokalemia
Hypertension onset before age 40
Adrenal incidentaloma with hypertension
Family history of early hypertension or stroke

hypothyroidism.description: Insufficient thyroid hormone production causing metabolic slowdown. Extremely common but frequently overlooked as a secondary cause of hypertension, weight gain, and fatigue.
red_flags: Fatigue with cold intolerance and weight gain
Diastolic hypertension with elevated cholesterol
Hair thinning with dry skin
Depression not responding to antidepressants
Family history of thyroid disease

systemic_lupus.description: Systemic autoimmune disease with average diagnostic delay of 6 years. Presents with fluctuating multi-system symptoms frequently attributed to stress, fibromyalgia, or depression.
red_flags: Young woman with joint pain, fatigue, and rash
Butterfly rash across cheeks and nose bridge
Multiple system involvement (joints + kidneys + skin)
Symptoms worsen with sun exposure
Recurrent oral ulcers with fatigue

early_rheumatoid_arthritis.description: Chronic inflammatory joint disease where early detection dramatically improves outcomes. Often initially dismissed as mechanical joint pain or fibromyalgia.

xray_hands_feet.normal_range: No erosions or joint space narrowing

red_flags: Morning stiffness lasting more than 1 hour
Symmetric swelling of small joints (MCP, PIP)
Joint symptoms persisting more than 6 weeks
Elevated inflammatory markers (CRP/ESR)
Family history of autoimmune disease

sjogrens_syndrome.description: Autoimmune exocrinopathy causing dry eyes and mouth. Average diagnostic delay of 4 years due to non-specific symptoms attributed to aging, medications, or anxiety.
red_flags: Persistent dry eyes and dry mouth
Recurrent dental cavities without explanation
Fatigue with joint pain in middle-aged woman
Parotid gland enlargement
Positive ANA with sicca symptoms

antiphospholipid_syndrome.description: Autoimmune thrombophilia causing recurrent clots and pregnancy losses. Frequently presents as resistant hypertension or recurrent cardiovascular events without recognized underlying cause.
red_flags: Unexplained arterial or venous thrombosis under age 50
Recurrent pregnancy loss (2+ miscarriages)
Livedo reticularis (net-like skin discoloration)
Stroke in young patient without traditional risk factors
Thrombocytopenia with clotting history

pheochromocytoma.description: Adrenal tumour secreting catecholamines causing episodic hypertension, sweating and headaches — frequently mistaken for treatment-resistant hypertension.
red_flags: Paroxysmal hypertensive episodes (headache, sweating, palpitations triad)
Hypertension refractory to 3+ antihypertensives
Hypertensive crisis triggered by anaesthesia, surgery, or medication
Adrenal incidentaloma on imaging
Family history of MEN2 or VHL syndrome

graves_disease.description: Autoimmune hyperthyroidism causing tachycardia, hypertension, and weight loss — commonly misattributed to anxiety or cardiovascular disease.
red_flags: Unintentional weight loss despite normal or increased appetite
Resting heart rate persistently > 100 bpm
Exophthalmos (bulging eyes) or pretibial myxedema
Atrial fibrillation in patient under 60 without known cause
Heat intolerance and excessive sweating

obstructive_sleep_apnea.description: Intermittent upper airway obstruction during sleep causing chronic hypoxia — a major unrecognised driver of resistant hypertension and metabolic syndrome.
red_flags: Chronic daytime fatigue unresponsive to adequate sleep
Witnessed apnoeas or loud snoring reported by partner
Morning headaches with neck circumference > 40 cm (women) / 43 cm (men)
Resistant hypertension despite 3+ antihypertensives
Nocturia (waking 2+ times per night to urinate)

atrial_fibrillation.description: Paroxysmal or persistent AF is frequently missed until a stroke occurs — hypertension and diabetes are the two leading modifiable risk factors.
red_flags: Palpitations, especially irregular or episodic
Unexplained fatigue or exercise intolerance
Stroke or TIA without identified cardioembolic source
Irregular pulse on clinical examination
Hypertension plus diabetes in patient over 55

hemochromatosis.description: Iron overload disorder causing diabetes, liver disease, and joint pain — often diagnosed only after significant organ damage has occurred.
red_flags: Diabetes with unexplained liver enzyme elevation (AST/ALT)
Bronze skin discoloration or hyperpigmentation
Arthropathy of 2nd and 3rd MCP joints (unusual distribution)
Unexplained fatigue in Northern European descent patient
Hypogonadism or loss of libido alongside metabolic symptoms

nafld_nash.description: Non-alcoholic fatty liver disease is the most common liver disease globally, frequently undetected until cirrhosis in patients with metabolic syndrome.
red_flags: Incidentally elevated ALT/AST in metabolic syndrome patient
Obesity with right upper quadrant discomfort
Diabetes poorly controlled despite medication adherence
Dyslipidaemia with low HDL and high triglycerides
Thrombocytopenia in obese patient (may indicate cirrhosis)

polymyalgia_rheumatica.description: Inflammatory condition of shoulder and pelvic girdle muscles in patients over 50 — misdiagnosed as degenerative arthritis or fibromyalgia for over a year on average.
red_flags: Bilateral shoulder and hip girdle stiffness > 45 minutes each morning
Difficulty raising arms above shoulder height
Rapid response to low-dose prednisolone (diagnostic)
New headache or jaw claudication (giant cell arteritis complication)
Profound fatigue with markedly elevated CRP in patient over 50

polycythemia_vera.description: Myeloproliferative disorder causing blood hyperviscosity, hypertension, and thrombosis — often found incidentally or after a stroke/DVT.
red_flags: Facial plethora (reddish complexion) with hypertension
Aquagenic pruritus (itching after hot shower)
Thrombosis at unusual sites (portal, hepatic, splenic vein)
Splenomegaly on examination or imaging
Unexplained elevated haematocrit on routine blood test

vitamin_d_deficiency.description: Clinically significant vitamin D deficiency is a modifiable driver of fatigue, musculoskeletal pain, and cardiometabolic risk — routinely overlooked in standard workups.
red_flags: Chronic fatigue and musculoskeletal pain in high-latitude or indoor-lifestyle patient
Recurrent respiratory infections suggesting immune suppression
Depression with physical fatigue unresponsive to antidepressants
Bone pain or low-trauma fractures
Dark skin tone, obesity, or limited sun exposure (major risk factors)

celiac_disease.description: Autoimmune gluten enteropathy causing fatigue, anaemia, and nutritional deficiencies — the longest diagnostic delay of any common autoimmune disease.
red_flags: Chronic fatigue with iron-deficiency anaemia unresponsive to oral iron
Peripheral neuropathy of unknown cause
Osteoporosis in young patient without other risk factors
Dermatitis herpetiformis (blistering rash)
Type 1 diabetes or autoimmune thyroid disease (strong association)

renal_artery_stenosis.description: Narrowing of the renal arteries causing renovascular hypertension — a correctable secondary cause of treatment-resistant hypertension frequently attributed to essential HTN.

renal_doppler_us.normal_range: Peak systolic velocity < 180 cm/s, resistive index < 0.8

red_flags: Hypertension onset before age 30 or abrupt worsening after age 55
Resistant hypertension despite 3+ drug regimen
Renal function deterioration after starting ACE inhibitor or ARB
Abdominal bruit on auscultation
Hypertension with atherosclerotic disease in other vascular beds
